user_query:
Please set up a small file-triggered automation on my local machine for upcoming WWII tour bookings. I’m a WWII museum tour guide and I want this to kick in whenever a new bookings CSV is added to a folder.

What I need:
1) Configure and implement the automation
- Use the provided config/automation.yaml and scripts/watcher.py. Update both so that the script watches the folder inbox/ for new CSV files named bookings_YYYY-MM-DD.csv and only processes each file once using the state file at state/processed.json.
- Fill in museum_name and tone in config/automation.yaml with reasonable values reflecting an energetic but respectful style. Keep message_max_sentences at 3, but ensure it is actually used by the automation to keep messages concise.

2) Extract structured data
- When a new CSV is detected in inbox/, parse it to extract tour_id, date, time, group_name, group_size, focus, contact_email, notes.
- Also parse input/exhibits.html to build a mapping of focus topics to exhibit names (use the first two exhibits listed under each relevant section as highlights). Do not hardcode exhibit names; extract them from the HTML file.
- Save a structured JSON per CSV at out/structured/bookings_YYYY-MM-DD.json that, for each booking, includes: tour_id, date, time, group_name, group_size, focus, contact_email, raw_notes, and highlights (the two exhibit names for that focus from the HTML).

3) Rewrite concise confirmation messages for each booking
- For each booking, generate two files under out/notifications/<tour_id>/: subject.txt and body.txt.
- The subject must include the museum_name, date, and time (e.g., "City War Museum Tour Confirmation – 2024-11-04 10:00").
- The body must be rewritten from any draft in the CSV notes (strip the leading "Draft msg:" text if present) into an energetic, clear tone guided by config/automation.yaml, and include: museum_name, group_name, date, time, group_size, and 1–2 relevant exhibits from the extracted highlights. Keep the body to at most the configured message_max_sentences.
- If notes include the word "Accessibility" (case-insensitive), append one concise sentence acknowledging accessibility arrangements.

4) Prepare meeting notes with action items
- Use input/resources.yaml to map each booking’s focus topic to preparatory items (e.g., print handouts, check audio). Where a prep item implies quantities, scale them to the group_size when obviously numeric (e.g., "Print N puzzle handouts").
- Create one meeting notes file per date at out/notes/meeting_notes_YYYY-MM-DD.md with:
  - A "Tours" section listing each tour_id with time, group_name, group_size, focus, and the two exhibit highlights.
  - An "Action Items" section listing per booking the focus-specific prep tasks (scaled by group_size where applicable), each as a checklist item.

Verification and run:
- To simulate the trigger in this workspace, the automation should detect a new file when you copy input/bookings_2024-11-04.csv into inbox/ and run scripts/watcher.py. It must create:
  - out/structured/bookings_2024-11-04.json
  - out/notifications/T-1001/subject.txt and body.txt; out/notifications/T-1002/subject.txt and body.txt; out/notifications/T-1003/subject.txt and body.txt
  - out/notes/meeting_notes_2024-11-04.md and out/notes/meeting_notes_2024-11-05.md
  - Update state/processed.json so re-running does not duplicate outputs.
- Do not rely on internet access. All data must come from the provided files. Make sure both the code and the config are modified so the automation actually runs and produces the outputs exactly under the specified paths. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
